items.name: Omaha Beach
items.extraction_note: Beach sector where visibility, mislandings, and radio loss changed command control.

items.name: Carentan causeway
source: The Battle of Carentan (8-15 June): Utah Beach to Cherbourg
items.extraction_note: Narrow route that canalized movement and exposed the attackers to flanking fire.

items.name: Bocage country
items.extraction_note: Hedgerow terrain that sharply reduced visibility and maneuver freedom.

items.name: Mont Ormel
items.extraction_note: Falaise-pocket hilltop that became a decisive memory and command-pressure node.

items.name: Villers-Bocage
items.extraction_note: Road- and hedgerow-constrained battlefield illustrating surprise and command collapse.

items.name: Bayeux
items.extraction_note: German reports around Bayeux were late/scattered, showing command-picture lag.
